title: "phrasplit Installation"

nav_tool: phrasplit

<!-- GENERATED by sphinxpress. Do not edit by hand. -->

<section id="installation">
<h1>Installation</h1>
<section id="requirements">
<h2>Requirements</h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>Python 3.9 or higher</p></li>
<li><p>click 8.0 or higher</p></li>
<li><p>rich 13.0 or higher</p></li>
<li><p>spaCy 3.5 or higher (optional, for high-accuracy mode)</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="installation-options">
<h2>Installation Options</h2>
<p>phrasplit can be installed with or without spaCy support, depending on your needs.</p>
<section id="basic-installation-lightweight">
<h3>Basic Installation (Lightweight)</h3>
<p>For a lightweight installation without spaCy (uses regex-based splitting):</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>pip<span class="w"> </span>install<span class="w"> </span>phrasplit
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>This installation is sufficient for basic text splitting and has no heavy dependencies.</p>
</section>
<section id="full-installation-with-spacy-recommended">
<h3>Full Installation with spaCy (Recommended)</h3>
<p>For higher accuracy sentence splitting with spaCy NLP:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>pip<span class="w"> </span>install<span class="w"> </span>phrasplit<span class="o">[</span>nlp<span class="o">]</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Or install spaCy separately:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>pip<span class="w"> </span>install<span class="w"> </span>phrasplit
pip<span class="w"> </span>install<span class="w"> </span>spacy&gt;<span class="o">=</span><span class="m">3</span>.5.0
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
</section>
<section id="installing-from-source">
<h2>Installing from Source</h2>
<p>To install from source, clone the repository and install:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>git<span class="w"> </span>clone<span class="w"> </span>https://github.com/buchwandler/phrasplit.git
<span class="nb">cd</span><span class="w"> </span>phrasplit
pip<span class="w"> </span>install<span class="w"> </span>-e<span class="w"> </span>.
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>For development, install with dev dependencies:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>pip<span class="w"> </span>install<span class="w"> </span>-e<span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;.[dev]&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>For spaCy support when installing from source:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>pip<span class="w"> </span>install<span class="w"> </span>-e<span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;.[nlp]&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="installing-spacy-language-models">
<h2>Installing spaCy Language Models</h2>
<p>If you installed with spaCy support, you’ll need a language model. The default model is
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">en_core_web_sm</span></code> (English). Install it with:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>python<span class="w"> </span>-m<span class="w"> </span>spacy<span class="w"> </span>download<span class="w"> </span>en_core_web_sm
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>For better accuracy, you can use larger models:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># Medium model (more accurate)</span>
python<span class="w"> </span>-m<span class="w"> </span>spacy<span class="w"> </span>download<span class="w"> </span>en_core_web_md

<span class="c1"># Large model (most accurate)</span>
python<span class="w"> </span>-m<span class="w"> </span>spacy<span class="w"> </span>download<span class="w"> </span>en_core_web_lg
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>For other languages, see the <a class="reference external" href="https://spacy.io/models">spaCy models documentation</a>.</p>
</section>
<section id="verifying-installation">
<h2>Verifying Installation</h2>
<p>You can verify your installation by running:</p>
<div class="highlight-python notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="kn">import</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">phrasplit</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">phrasplit</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">__version__</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">phrasplit</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">split_sentences</span>

<span class="c1"># Works with or without spaCy</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">split_sentences</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;Hello world. How are you?&quot;</span><span class="p">))</span>
<span class="c1"># [&#39;Hello world.&#39;, &#39;How are you?&#39;]</span>

<span class="c1"># Explicitly use simple mode (no spaCy required)</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">split_sentences</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;Hello world. How are you?&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">use_spacy</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">False</span><span class="p">))</span>
<span class="c1"># [&#39;Hello world.&#39;, &#39;How are you?&#39;]</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="choosing-between-modes">
<h2>Choosing Between Modes</h2>
<p><strong>Simple Mode (use_spacy=False)</strong></p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>No spaCy installation required</p></li>
<li><p>Faster processing</p></li>
<li><p>Lower memory usage</p></li>
<li><p>Good for straightforward text</p></li>
<li><p>Uses regex-based splitting</p></li>
</ul>
<p><strong>spaCy Mode (use_spacy=True, default if available)</strong></p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>Requires spaCy and language models</p></li>
<li><p>Higher accuracy</p></li>
<li><p>Better handling of complex cases</p></li>
<li><p>Uses NLP-based analysis</p></li>
<li><p>Recommended for production use</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>
